title: NewLimit closes $435M Series C, sets first-in-human aging reprogramming trial for 2027
description: Brian Armstrong's NewLimit closed a $435M Series C to bring its lead epigenetic reprogramming therapy — a partial reprogramming drug for aged human liver cells — into a Phase 1 clinical trial in alcohol-related liver disease in 2027. The round, one of the largest ever for a pure-play longevity company, marks a major shift from preclinical bench work to actual human longevity drug development.

title: UC San Diego: first RCT shows GLP-1 semaglutide slows DunedinPACE aging clock by 9%
description: In a Nature Communications paper, UC San Diego's Corley lab ran the first randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of semaglutide as an anti-aging intervention in 108 HIV patients. After 32 weeks, semaglutide slowed biological aging by 9% on DunedinPACE and reduced PCGrimAge mortality-risk markers across inflammation, brain, heart, kidney, liver and metabolic clocks — the strongest clinical evidence yet that GLP-1 drugs may broadly influence aging biology.

title: Sinclair publishes first comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions
description: David Sinclair (Harvard) and Adiv Johnson published the first comprehensive evidence map cataloging human studies of interventions targeting epigenetic aging — including methylation clocks, partial reprogramming, NAD+ boosters, senolytics and GLP-1s. The map gives clinicians and biotechs a shared scorecard for which interventions actually have human RCT evidence today.

title: Cyclarity UDP-003 Phase 1: first-in-human oxidized cholesterol excretion data unveiled
description: Cyclarity Therapeutics presented Phase 1 data at AHA Vascular Discovery showing UDP-003 — a cyclodextrin targeting toxic 7-ketocholesterol inside macrophages and soft plaques — produced measurable oxidized cholesterol excretion in humans. This is the first clinical proof of concept for clearing the root atherogenic lipid driver, not just lowering LDL.

title: Biotech ETFs sell off hard for second straight session: XBI -4.4%, SBIO -4.7%
description: Despite Lilly's vaccine M&A wave and NewLimit's $435M raise, longevity-adjacent biotech ETFs continued their selloff Tuesday with XBI -4.39%, SBIO -4.73%, IBB -3.01%, ARKG -2.16% and BBH -2.26%. The two-day cumulative decline signals risk-off rotation away from speculative biotech even as breakthrough longevity capital flows in.

title: NewLimit raises $435M Series C for aging reprogramming therapy
description: South San Francisco's NewLimit, co-founded by Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong, plans to launch its first clinical trial of a liver medicine after raising $435M in new funding, targeting first-in-human dosing in 2027.

title: AllSci: NewLimit $435M Series C details aging reprogramming pipeline
description: AllSci breaks down NewLimit's $435M Series C: capital earmarked to advance the prototype liver-cell reprogramming medicine into first-in-human trials, with a multi-asset partial reprogramming pipeline behind it.

title: UC San Diego: GLP-1 semaglutide slows biological aging in randomized trial
description: Researchers at UC San Diego published the first randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical evidence that semaglutide slows biological aging markers in DNA across multiple epigenetic clocks in adults with HIV-associated lipohypertrophy, with a 9% DunedinPACE deceleration.

title: Sinclair and Adiv Johnson publish first comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions
description: David Sinclair and Adiv Johnson released the first comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions, building a clinician-ready scorecard of interventions tested in humans.

title: Yahoo Finance covers Sinclair-Johnson human epigenetic aging evidence map
description: Yahoo Finance amplifies the Sinclair-Johnson evidence map publication, positioning it as a structured resource for investors and clinicians evaluating epigenetic aging interventions in humans.

title: Bar-Ilan Sirt6 paper continues to ripple: rejuvenation of liver cells confirmed
description: Global Longevity Federation highlights the Bar-Ilan Sirt6 paper showing molecular signatures of youth restored in aged mouse liver cells, validating Sirt6 as a key target for SirTLab and other geroprotector programs.

title: Cyclarity unveils UDP-003 oxidized cholesterol excretion data at AHA Vascular Discovery
description: Cyclarity Therapeutics presented Phase 1 data at AHA Vascular Discovery Scientific Sessions for UDP-003, a cyclodextrin targeting toxic 7-ketocholesterol — the first clinical proof of concept for clearing oxidized cholesterol from macrophages and soft plaques.

title: Lifespan Rejuvenation Roundup May 2026: senescence, mitochondria and stem cells take center stage
description: Lifespan.io's monthly digest features primary vs secondary senescence biology, phosphatidylcholine decline in mitochondrial aging, time-restricted feeding restoring stem cell function, and a methylene blue protection signal for hair follicle stem cells.

title: Longevity.Technology Week 22 roundup: Sam Altman's $1.8B Retro, FDA-cleared sleep diagnostic, and longevity clinic accountability
description: This week's Longevity.Technology roundup covers Retro Biosciences hitting $1.8B valuation, Sunrise Air FDA-cleared at-home sleep apnea diagnostic, Scribe Therapeutics' 18-month CRISPR cholesterol reduction, and Milan Longevity Summit pushing clinic outcome accountability.

title: Forever Healthy releases AI4L 1.0 open-source AI for practical longevity reviews
description: Forever Healthy launched AI4L 1.0 ('AI for Practical Longevity'), an open-source system that lets anyone produce evidence-based reviews of health and longevity interventions using frontier AI models — a significant step toward democratizing longevity protocol literature reviews.

title: China launches first national competency-based longevity medicine education programme
description: China launched its first national competency-based education programme in longevity medicine, integrating healthy longevity and preventive care into mainstream clinical practice and signaling state-scale adoption of geroscience clinical training.

title: PaisanoAI Healthspan Update May 31 – June 13, 2026 newsletter
description: PaisanoAI's bi-weekly Healthspan Update synthesizes the latest geroscience, AI in longevity, and clinical protocol news in a single curated digest for the practitioner audience.

title: Superpower: berberine extends lifespan 15-20% in C. elegans via AMPK
description: A Superpower-published deep dive on berberine compiles AMPK-activation lifespan data: 15-20% extension in C. elegans, ~30% extension in Drosophila, plus cognitive and neuroinflammation benefits in aged mice.

title: Sirt6 reverses liver aging in mice — Bar-Ilan paper widely re-covered
description: Bar-Ilan University and partner labs continue to attract press for the Sirt6 paper showing reversal of liver aging in mice via SIRT6 elevation, with SirTLab developing small-molecule activators.

title: Wall Street Journal — Inside Putin's $26B quest for longevity
description: WSJ investigation details Russia's $26B Kremlin-backed longevity and organ-replacement program, including the Beijing hot-mic exchange between Putin and Xi.

title: NMN.com: PEARL trial — first long-term human rapamycin RCT for muscle and bone
description: NMN.com's report on the PEARL trial confirms no serious adverse events over 48 weeks of weekly rapamycin in older adults; 10 mg/week preserved muscle in women and bone mineral content in men.

title: Tohoku University launches HealthSpan Research Center (HeSReC)
description: Japan's Tohoku University launched the HealthSpan Research Center (HeSReC) to promote healthy aging via translational geroscience and clinical implementation programs.

title: McGill: novel longevity therapy and fall prevention strategy findings
description: A new McGill University study may pave the way for novel longevity therapies and enhanced fall-prevention strategies in older adults, with mobility-axis mechanism details forthcoming.

title: Peter Attia podcast #394: sleep pharmacology and Alzheimer's prevention via DORAs
description: Peter Attia's new episode dives into sleep pharmacology, classes of prescription sleep meds, their effects on sleep architecture, and the promise that newer dual orexin receptor antagonists (DORAs) may hold for Alzheimer's prevention in high-risk patients.

title: Bryan Johnson floats biological-age study of crypto industry workers
description: Bryan Johnson publicly proposed measuring the biological age of cryptocurrency-industry workers to test whether the high-stress sector accelerates aging — a likely follow-on to his Blueprint marketing playbook.

title: News Medical: mammals share universal gene signatures as they age
description: A study covered by News Medical reports that mammals share universal gene signatures as they age — potential 'molecular clocks' that could help extend healthy lifespans across species.

title: Aging May One Day Be Treated From the Cell Up — senolytics primer goes mainstream
description: Mainstream consumer coverage of senolytics continues to expand, with Engineering Post summarizing the senescent-cell-clearance class and noting emerging evidence on tissue health, muscle function and biological-age markers.

title: Langtree Group: Beyond the Blueprint — corporate longevity benefits emerging
description: Langtree's 'Beyond the Blueprint' analysis tracks employer-sponsored longevity benefits — diagnostics, supplement stacks, and protocol coaching — moving from boutique perks into mainstream HR packages.

title: Nature long-read: 'How long can humans live? We simply don't know'
description: Nature features Saul Newman's long-read arguing biomarker estimates of age must be calibrated against physical dating methods (amino-acid racemization, radiocarbon) to restore confidence in supercentenarian and mortality-plateau claims.

title: Fight Aging June 1 newsletter: ProAL50 allostatic-load index and multi-omics clocks
description: This week's Fight Aging digest leads with the ProAL50 proteomic allostatic-load index, multi-omics clock convergence, nanomaterials in osteoarthritis cartilage and the genetics of exceptional longevity.
